name: Vacuum Tube Electronics
tagline: Thermionic emission in evacuated envelopes — amplification and switching before solid-state junctions dominated
problem:
Early radio detectors used crystals but lacked stable gain; long-distance telephony needed repeaters; radar needed microsecond switching. Vacuum tubes provide gain by accelerating thermionic electrons across vacuum where space-charge laws let grids modulate current — a macroscopic valve analogous to modern transistors.

Obstacles include pump technology to reach hard vacuum, gettering to absorb residual gas, heater burnouts, microphonics from mechanical vibration, and kilowatt plate dissipation in transmitters. Tubes age as cathode emission drops and gas ionises.

They are heavy, hot, and fragile — but teach the control-grid concepts that solid-state devices inherit in different physics.
overview:
A heated cathode emits electrons; a positive plate collects them; intermediate grids accelerate or repel the cloud to modulate plate current. Triodes amplify; tetrodes and pentodes reduce Miller capacitance for RF. Cathode-ray tubes steer beams with electric and magnetic fields for oscilloscopes and television displays.

Manufacturing glass-to-metal seals join leads; getters flash to bind stray gas; mica or ceramic spacers locate grids with micron precision. Pump trains use rotary roughing pumps plus diffusion or turbomolecular high vacuum.

Power tubes need graphite or metal anodes that radiate heat; forced air or water jackets prevent glass softening. Bias supplies and screen grids set operating points on load lines — design is graphical and empirical before SPICE.

Military radar and early computers consumed tube populations measured in tens of thousands per machine — logistics rivalling ammunition.

principles.name: Child–Langmuir law
principles.explanation: Space-charge-limited current scales as three-halves power of plate voltage in planar diode idealisations.

principles.name: Transconductance
principles.explanation: Small-signal plate current change per grid voltage change sets gain with chosen plate load resistance.

principles.name: Miller effect
principles.explanation: Plate-to-grid capacitance multiplies effective input capacitance — motivates tetrode/pentode and later neutralisation in RF amps.

principles.name: Secondary emission
principles.explanation: High-energy electrons knock secondaries from grids — causes negative resistance regions exploited in dynodes but parasitic in tetrodes without suppressor grids.

components.name: Heated cathode
components.function: Thermionic emitter coated with oxides for low work function

components.name: Control and screen grids
components.madeFrom: Molybdenum wire wound on notched rods

components.name: Anode plate
components.madeFrom: Nickel or graphite with radiation fins in power tubes

components.name: Getter ring
components.function: Chemically pumps residual gas after seal-off

buildSteps.title: Fabricate electrode stack
buildSteps.description: Wind grids with uniform pitch; align coaxially; spot-weld supports; coat cathode and carbonate.

buildSteps.title: Seal envelope
buildSteps.description: Flame or induction seal glass; leak-check with He mass spectrometer; electrical hi-pot inside oil bath.
buildSteps.warningNote: Pinholes flash over at high voltage — operator hazard

buildSteps.title: Pump and bake
buildSteps.description: Bake-out drives water from glass; reach high vacuum; flash getter.
buildSteps.warningNote: Implosion hazard — use safety shields during evacuation tests

buildSteps.title: Age and match
buildSteps.description: Run cathode at over-temperature briefly to stabilise emission; pair tubes for push-pull if needed.

buildSteps.title: Mark and bin
buildSteps.description: Print type numbers; sort by gm and noise for audio; log lot for warranty.

history.event: Fleming valve diode enables rectification for early radio.
De Forest Audion triode introduces grid control.
ENIAC-class machines deploy thousands of tubes for digital switching.

impact:
Tubes carried voice across continents, lit radar screens, and ran first computers. Their obsolescence in consumer gear does not erase their pedagogical clarity: visible electrodes, measurable potentials.

High-power RF transmitters still use descendants (klystrons, magnetrons) in niche roles.

videos.title: Vacuum tube operation
videos.description: Physics lectures on thermionic emission and triode curves.
